zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Wortley於1441年出生在英國越可爾沃特利時，她的父親Nicholas Wortley II41歲，母親Isabel Tunstall29歲。她於1470年與Sir Richard Woodroffe在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1478年在英國越可爾伍利享年37歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Wortley于1441年出生在英国越可爾沃特利时，她的父亲Nicholas Wortley II41岁，母亲Isabel Tunstall29岁。她于1470年与Sir Richard Woodroffe在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1478年在英国越可爾伍利享年37岁。

nameMeanings.name: Wortley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Wortley in Tankersley (Yorkshire), or Lower, Upper, or New Wortley in Armley (Yorkshire), or (least likely) Wortley in Wotton under Edge (Gloucestershire). The place in Tankersley derives from Old English wyrt ‘plant, vegetable’ + lēah ‘wood, woodland clearing’. The place in Armley derives from an Old English personal name Wyrca or Weorca + Old English lēah. The Gloucestershire placename derives from Old English wyrt + lēah.

variants.name: Worley
meanings: habitational name with different origins. In the West Midlands this surname is most probably derived from Great or Little Wyrley in Norton Canes (Staffordshire). The placenames derive from Old English wīr ‘bog myrtle’ + lēah ‘wood, woodland clearing’. In the East Midlands, the name is possibly from Worlick in Ramsey (Huntingdonshire), the etymology of the which is uncertain. In northern England, possibly from Whirley in Over Alderley (Cheshire), which has the same etymology as the Staffordshire names. There may also be a further unlocated source of this habitational name in southern England.
possibly an irregular variant of   Wortley   or   Warley  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Wortley was born in 1441, in Wortley, Yorkshire, England, her father, Nicholas Wortley II, was 41 and her mother, Isabel Tunstall, was 29. She married Sir Richard Woodroffe in 1470, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1478, in Woolley, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 37.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Wortley est née en 1441, à Wortley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Nicholas Wortley II, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Isabel Tunstall, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Richard Woodroffe en 1470, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1478, à Woolley, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 37 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Wortley 1441 in Wortley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Nicholas Wortley II 41 und ihre Mutter Isabel Tunstall 29. Sie hat 1470 in Yorkshire, England Sir Richard Woodroffe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1478 im Alter von 37 Jahren in Woolley, Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Wortley è nata il nel 1441 nel Wortley, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Nicholas Wortley II, aveva 41 anni e sua madre, Isabel Tunstall, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato Sir Richard Woodroffe nel 1470, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1478, nel Woolley, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 37 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Wortleyが1441年にイギリスヨークシュアイアウオルトリーで生まれた時，父Nicholas Wortley IIは41歳，母Isabel Tunstallは29歳であった。1470年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでSir Richard Woodroffeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1478年にイギリスヨークシュアイアウリーで37歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Wortley(이)가 1441년 영국 요르크실르 워틀리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas Wortley II(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Tunstall(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1470년 영국 요르크실르에서 Sir Richard Woodroffe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1478년 영국 요르크실르 보리에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Wortley nasceu em 1441, em Wortley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Nicholas Wortley II, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Tunstall, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Richard Woodroffe em 1470, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1478, em Woolley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 37 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Wortley родилась в 1441 г. в местности вортли, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Nicholas Wortley II было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Isabel Tunstall, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard Woodroffe в 1470 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1478 г. в местности вулли, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 37 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Wortley nació en 1441, en Wortley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Nicholas Wortley II, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Isabel Tunstall, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Sir Richard Woodroffe en 1470, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1478, en Woolley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 37 años.
